text: Just a few weeks ago, we saw some Modern
Audience developers on their Blue Sky
accounts talking about why there should
be more censorship on Steam. Those
gamers are too mean to the corporations.
And I mentioned how their whining
actually reminded me of Ubisoft around a
year ago when it was reported that
Ubisoft was looking into pressuring
Steam to hide the concurrent player
counts on games. They don't want people
to be able to see the concurrent player
count. And now over on Twitter, people
are once again crying about the Steam
player counts being public. Oh no data
as I saw Vera Dark cover this tweet from
February 28th that reads Steam player
account being public is the worst thing
to happen to gaming in recent time
mentality of if this game going in a
niche genre doesn't outperform every
game ever released its DOA. Like holy
smokes I can't put into words how
like holy smokes I can't put into words
how stupid it is and now it'll be every
game released. Man, I couldn't even
finish reading that post without
laughing. And that middle section is
such a ridiculous straw man. Like way
too overly dramatic. No one's saying it
has to beat every game ever when we're
taking a look at metrics like this. This
user would respond to the OP saying,
"Nah, player count is an important
metric to gauge a game, especially in
multi/competitive games." And the OP
would respond saying this. Player count,
which can change drastically with any
update at any point for any given game.
Not saying it's useless, but it sure
isn't a reliable measure. Well, that's
exactly the point. If the player count
suddenly shoots up or suddenly shoots
down, it's indicative of something else
occurring in the game. For example, a
major update or a patch that had bad
balancing, you know, stuff like that.
And people might then look further into
the data as well, like what caused this
spike or this decrease. By the way, it
looks like the OP is really just upset
about the recent Marathon discourse, as
the OP had also previously said this.
I'm so glad Marathon is one of the games
spearheading the why are you so
miserable and pathetic? You want
everything to fail dialogue. I'm so
tired of the direction the games
industry is going. The crowd wanting
everything to fail before it's even out
is the one killing innovation. Oh yeah,
because the AAA industry is so
innovative, right? Yeah. No. Moving on
though, we have the next OP where this
person had said, "I'm convinced a
petition should be started to shut down
Steam DB. We should not be allowed to
see player counts for any game anymore,
nor how many are viewing on Twitch." How
are these people real, man? Like imagine
actually advocating for less data, less
information. Oh no, the numbers are
offensive. That shouldn't be that
shouldn't be made public. At least it
makes for some hilarious content though.
And once again, credit to Verdark
because her coverage is how I found out
about these posts. Let me know what you
think about all this in the comments.
Thank you for tuning in and have a
lovely week ahead.
